meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Solving No Testing Knowledge
content.seo_description: Overcome a lack of testing knowledge using project management strategies. A beginner-friendly guide leveraging Antigravity and Claude Code to build apps.
content.answer_block: You don't need a QA degree to build robust software. By shifting your focus from 'writing tests' to 'managed functional requirements,' you can use AI-driven project management to automate your validation flow. Use Antigravity to structure your logic and Claude Code to verify your build against specific functional milestones.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a code editor is daunting, but the silence of a failing application is terrifying. For many students, the gap between writing code and knowing it works is a chasm filled with 'no testing knowledge.' You feel like an imposter because you can't guarantee your app won't break the moment a user clicks a button.

But here is the secret: testing isn't about complex frameworks; it's about clear, managed expectations. By treating your project architecture as a set of verifiable tasks, you turn the chaotic process of 'hoping it works' into a scientific process of 'verifying it works.' We are going to bridge that gap today.

steps.title: Define Functional Specs
steps.description: Before writing code, define exactly what success looks like. This replaces complex unit tests with clear, human-readable verification points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead Engineer. Create a functional specification document for a simple To-Do list app, outlining the 5 core user actions and their success criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a specs.md file based on the user stories defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Architect the Task Tree
steps.description: Break your project into granular tasks. This ensures you only build what you can verify, preventing scope creep and hidden bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert my functional specs into a hierarchical task tree. Ensure each task includes a 'Verification Step' that a non-expert could perform manually.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a tasks.json file that maps to the verification steps provided.'

steps.title: Implement with Guardrails
steps.description: Use your task list as a roadmap to feed code snippets into your project. This prevents you from building 'blind'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the boilerplate code for the first task in my tree, ensuring it includes a clear entry point and exit point for testing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the code for task 1 based on the Antigravity boilerplate.'

steps.title: Automate the Sanity Check
steps.description: Instead of writing complex test suites, use Claude Code to verify your output against your original functional specs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a prompt for Claude Code that compares my current code output against the 'Verification Step' defined in Task 1.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Verify the implementation of Task 1 against the specs.md success criteria.'

steps.title: Iterative Refinement
steps.description: Loop through remaining tasks, ensuring each one is verified before moving to the next. This creates a high-confidence development cycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current state of my project and suggest the next logical task, including potential edge cases I should account for.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute task 2 and perform a self-check against the requirements.'

pro_tips: Treat your 'Verification Steps' as documentation for your future self; they become the backbone of your portfolio's README.
Never build more than 30 minutes of code without running a verification check against your task list.
Use Claude Code to generate 'negative test' scenarios—ask it specifically what could make the code fail.
If a task is too complex to verify, break it down further; complexity is the enemy of reliability.
Document the 'Why' behind every code change; this is more valuable to employers than the code itself.
content.next_steps: Once you have completed your first app using this workflow, host it on Vercel or GitHub Pages and add a 'Verification Log' to your README to show recruiters your professional methodology.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to build confidently without formal testing training. Apply this system to your next project and watch your velocity and code quality skyrocket. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
